title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack sufficient context and memory to be effective, using an analogy of John Carmack unable to see the full codebase. He frames this as a critical limitation that needs solving and positions Nx and Polygraph as solutions for improving agent autonomy in monorepo environments.
writeup: Victor Savkin of Nx argues that current coding agents are limited by insufficient context and memory, comparing the situation to a genius like John Carmack who cannot see the full codebase. The talk frames this as a critical limitation for agent effectiveness and positions Nx and Polygraph as tools for improving agent autonomy in monorepo environments. The core takeaways: context window and persistent agent memory are the bottlenecks for autonomy, and monorepo management infrastructure is offered as the means to supply agents with the broader codebase awareness they lack.
